reasoning: The Lean statement uses `∃!` (ExistsUnique, defined in Mathlib as `∃ x, p x ∧ ∀ y, p y → y = x`) to assert there is exactly one h with g*h = 1 ∧ h*g = 1, which is the standard two-sided definition of "inverse" in group theory. The "in a group" context is captured by `{G : Type*} [Group G]`, and "every element" is captured by the implicit variable `{g : G}` which is universally quantified. Using the two-sided condition (both g*h = 1 and h*g = 1) matches the standard definition of an inverse in group theory. Thus the Lean signature is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
